entry.title: Bitcoin covenants are inevitable
entry.summary:
In recent discussions, there has been a noteworthy observation regarding the economics of blockchain transactions. A key point raised is that although the cost per transaction appears high when dividing the total block reward by the number of transactions, this perspective shifts when considering the average value transferred per transaction. Typically, thousands of dollars are moved in each transaction, which contextualizes the seemingly high costs as relatively affordable due to the substantial amounts involved. This insight provides a deeper understanding of the inflationary impact on transaction costs within blockchain systems.
For more detailed information on blockchain economies and related insights, Peter Todd offers extensive resources and analyses, which can be accessed [here](https://petertodd.org).
